title: cruddasia craibii
intro: Cruddasia craibii is a rare and endemic tree species found in the Philippines, belonging to the family Sapindaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its bark and leaves. The health benefits of Cruddasia craibii include its potential in treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its antiseptic properties, which make it useful in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and can also be used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
